title: linum suffruticosum var. squarrosum
intro: Linum suffruticosum var. squarrosum, also known as creeping flax, is a perennial plant in the flax family (Linaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of creeping flax include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and support wound healing. Creeping flax can be prepared as an herbal infusion or tincture, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
